papers.name: Paper 2 Short Answer and Structured
papers.coverage_notes: Short-answer and structured questions; includes studies and research methods.

assessment_objectives.name: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding
assessment_objectives.description: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of psychological concepts, theories, research methods and applications.
classification_hints: Items asking for definitions, terminology, recall of theories and studies.

assessment_objectives.name: Apply knowledge and understanding
assessment_objectives.description: Apply psychological knowledge and understanding to a range of contexts.
classification_hints: Items requiring application of psychology to scenarios, evaluation of studies.

assessment_objectives.name: Analyse, evaluate and draw conclusions
assessment_objectives.description: Analyse, evaluate and draw conclusions from psychological information and arguments.
classification_hints: Items requiring analysis, evaluation of methodology, weighing up of evidence.

question_styles.name: Multiple choice
question_styles.description: Tests breadth of knowledge across the syllabus.

question_styles.name: Case study / scenario
question_styles.description: Interpretation of psychological scenarios and studies.

difficulty_ladder.description: Recall of facts, definitions or single-step application.

lesson_use.notes: Set supported=true only when a teacher/student/parent mode is declared and validated.

marking_guidance.command_words_ref: CAIE IGCSE Psychology command words (state, describe, explain, outline, discuss, evaluate) are defined in Section 4 of the syllabus.
marking_guidance.notes: Single-tier qualification (grades A*-G). Both papers required.
